publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-07 18:03:25 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, manuscripts, printed materials, financial and legal records, and lantern slides.
did.abstract: Correspondence, manuscripts, printed materials, regarding his professional career and financial accounts pertaining to Ithaca, N.Y. organizations. Additional records include deeds, mortgage and survey (1733-1803) on lands in Albany and Montgomery Counties and newspaper clippings and handwritten notes about the early Ithaca "Moral Society". One box of lantern slides.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of American History, Cornell University. Hull died in 1936.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence of Hull relating to his studies in Germany in the 1890's, to his work as professor and dean at Cornell University, to his research as an historian, and to the professional organizations to which he belonged, particularly the American Historical Association; also manuscript and printed materials, correspondence and financial accounts pertaining to Ithaca, N.Y. organizations such as the Cornell Public Library, the Cornell Heights Land Company, the Fall Creek Milling Company, the Ithaca Children's Home, the Ithaca Band, and the Ladies Union Benevolent Society. Additional records include deeds, mortgage and survey (1733-1803) on lands in Albany and Montgomery Counties and newspaper clippings and handwritten notes about the early Ithaca "Moral Society"; a few letters and other papers of Miss Mary J. Hull, sister of Professor Hull; English documents(1607-1725). Also included is correspondence of William A. Dunning, Max Farrand, Charles Homer Haskins, J. Franklin Jameson, Waldo G. Leland, Andrew C. McLaughlin, Isaac P. Roberts, Henry Morse Stephens, Willard Straight, James Sullivan, Hendrik Willem Van Loon. One box of lantern slides.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Charles Henry Hull papers, #14-17-249. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: History -- Study and teaching.
History -- Societies, etc.
History -- Research.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon Copy of letter from Webster of the Cornell Democratic Club re: its sponsorship of a lecture by William J. Bryan on "A Conquering Nation" at the Lyceum 'Theatre, March 6, 1912, and urging the Cornell faculty to attend.

did.unittitle: Ithaca Band Fund Committee to Hull

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Bement, Carpenter, Todd and Wellar) about fund raising. Mimeo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Steward, Treman, Rothschild, Bouton and Priest) regarding testimonials for Randolph Horton and Henry A. St. John, retiring Ithaca Mayor, and superintendent of Public Works, respectively. Typed or Mimeo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two page mimeo letter. Requesting subscribers of the new "City Hospital to express their preference for or against the use of the present site and enclosing a summary statement stating why he thinks the present site is not the best one available.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Historical Association letterhead; routine AHA re: signing a paper; return of reprints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: AHA Hull, Dunning and Bowen on prize essay committee; pros & cons of different methods of publication. 3 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Details of prize essay discussion; financial and publication problems. 5 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: AHA re: publications; report draft by Bowen - will Hull present it to Council at Madison?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hull agrees to present report at Madison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Can't find a publisher for essays; printing of the essays is more important than the prize money

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Hull's having asked someone (possibly Joseph G. de Roulhac Hamilton) to lecture at Cornell; Dunning suggests on "some phase of Reconstruction History." Also setting limit of 100,000 words on prize essay; Henry Holt mentioned as wanting to set up publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Dunning's letter about Hamilton (Apr. 25); agrees about 100,000 word limit, with conditions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional Charles H. Hull Papers. These were found among the Burr-White materials in a large envelope with the following notation in George Lincoln Burr's handwriting: "Hull's correspondence in connection with the Bancroft episode of the American Historical Association. He has more which he will add when he has sifted it from his other papers. G.L.B."

did.unittitle: John H. Latane to McLaughlin (Andrew C.) (Open Letter) (copy)
Circular letter to the members of the American Historical Association
Katharine Berry Judson ('04) to Hull (not on Bancroft episode)
Draft of Circular Letter to members of the American Historical Association
Draft of telegram Hull to McLaughlin
Hull to the members of the committee of nine of the American Historical Association (copy)
A.C. McLaughlin to Charles H. Hull
Report of the Committee of Nine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appointed to Consider the Constitution, Organization and Procedure of the American Historical association and the Relationships between the Association and the American Historical Review.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Columbia Univ. letterhead re: a matter of publication; reference to McLaughlin /"that gang"/ Leland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Withholding McLaughlin letter from Leland; hopes to see Farrand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Wash. mtg. -- McLaughlin will not attend, Farrand will.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Conference held.

did.unittitle: Howard Cobb to Mary J. Hull

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Gifts and endowments of the Children's Home, Ithaca

did.unittitle: Note on George M. Bartlett will
Newman and Adam to Children's Home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Gifts and endowments of the Children's Home, Ithaca

did.unittitle: Mary J. Hull to Bert T. Baker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Gifts and endowments of the Children's Home, Ithaca

did.unittitle: George H. Russell to Mary J. Hull

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Re: Gifts and endowments of the Children's Home, Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dealing with the Hull family, Cornell and Ithaca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed A. M. Hull, Ithaca, Tompkins County, N. Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to her from Aunt Gazena, Christmas

did.unittitle: Notes and Expenses of European Travel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed in Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William B. Cleveland, Charles Hull, R. H. Thurston, and C. K. Adams, and a sketch of H. Morse Stephens. Also, photograph captioned "The Irish Braking Ground," as early Cornell quarry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small black box

did.unittitle: Series II. Inventory of English Documents
Admission of Thomas Moore, Shelton Hall - Latin
Indenture, Charles Foyell, Nathaniel Brown, Edward Anderson, Thomas Hooke, plaintiffs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fine against: Christopher Bunn and Fayth, his wife; Richard Braben and Anne, his wife; (Lenny) Goodale and (Feberra), his wife. Transfer of land.

did.unittitle: Exemption of a fine, restoration of land confiscated in Cowton to Arthur Hulton Defore
Forfeit of tenure of land in Huttington by William Smith to William, Lord of the Manor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grant of tenure to land to William Smith and his wife, Alice, for the yearly sum of two shillings and other customs

did.unittitle: Lease for half of Birch Hall, R. Higgenbotham to Robert Hadfield
Lease of land in South Cowton, York, to Thomas Pepper by Robert, Earl of Ailesbury
Indenture between Thomas Smithson of Moulton, York, and Hugh Smithson, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchase of an annuity by Hugh from Thomas. 200 paid for 20 a year for eighteen years, guaranteed by lands in Moulton

did.unittitle: List of leases issued by Sir Marmaduke Gresham to John Gresham, gentleman
Provision by Jerome Moore for the support of Adrian (Adria), his wife, and the education of his two children
Indenture, sale of land by James Smithurst to Abraham Haworth, Ashley, Manchester
Indenture, sale of land in South Cowton, York, by Cuthbert Pepper to Thomas Smithson and Layford Wainwright
Indenture, release of land by Phillip Folla to John Folla, St. Michaels
Warrant against Robert Batemen of Middleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Warrant, Bartholow Holmesfield to Johnathan Johnson, George Davenport, and Hugh Bateman, Attorneys at Her Majesty's Court of Common Pleas at Westminster. Attachment in Latin

did.unittitle: John Isall, William Goodeman, Crow, John Moore, Redenhall - Latin

Robert Crow, woolen draper, appoints Richard Sutton, Nicholas Cordey, and Thomas Freeman, woolen drapers, and Anthony (Kerruk), brewer, to administer his land tenure on Redenhall Manor, (Harleston), Norfolk
Five copies of a will, Henry Whilbey to Pepper, Esquire, South Cowton, Latin.
Will of Daniel Cooke, bequest of land in Fulham to his youngest son, John
Estate bought of John Andrews, Latin
Series III. Inventory of American Documents
Photograph of a $30 bill, issued November 2, 1776 by the United Colonies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Troop of the Horse, Montgomery County, New York State Militia; issued to Daniel Visscher

did.unittitle: Invoice, Robert Smith to A.H. Taylor, Centerville Post Office, Bibb County, Alabama

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bill for household and farm articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assignment of lands held by Garret Vanderbilt, (of the New York, Militia, Captain Cooper's Company, War of 1812) to Charles R. McNulty, 120 acres of public land in district of land subject to sale at Fort Dodge, Iowa

did.unittitle: Deed, Ezra Cornell and his wife to William P. Pope, Harvey May, Albert M. Hull, and Alfred C. Pope

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sale of land along Fall Creek, Village of Ithaca. Transferred to Box I, Hull Papers

did.unittitle: Bound volume, Fall Creek Milling Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of the organizational meeting. Includes: copy of the Certificate of Incorporation of the Fall Creek Milling Company, By-Laws of the Fall Creek Milling Company, Minutes of the Board of Directors. Minutes of the Meetings of the Stockholders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also Acc. #39A , 1621

did.unittitle: Metal jewelry box with contents (one infant's. slipper, one carved pendant, one string blue beads, one carved horse head)
